description_long: This is a bulbous ceramic vase with a wide, rounded body and a narrow, flared neck, featuring a single handle attached from the neck to the shoulder. The surface is adorned with intricate geometric patterns, including zigzags, crosshatching, and bands of triangles, rendered in dark brown or black against a pale, off-white background. The neck and rim display fine, diagonal hatching, while the body is encircled by bold, angular motifs and a central band of repeating triangular designs. The handle is robust and slightly curved, blending seamlessly into the vessel's form. The vase has a matte, slightly rough finish, suggesting it is hand-crafted and possibly aged. Subtle discolorations and surface marks indicate some wear, adding to its antique character.
description: A rounded ceramic vase with a flared neck, single handle, and intricate geometric patterns in dark brown on an off-white surface. The vessel has a matte, slightly worn finish and a hand-crafted appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flared neck, detailed geometric patterns, and the full rounded body with no handle visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the single curved handle attached to the neck and shoulder, with geometric patterns wrapping around the body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the opposite side of the neck and body, with the handle not visible and the geometric motifs clearly seen.
description_view_3: Angled view showing both the handle and the flared neck, with the intricate surface patterns and some visible wear.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museo Nacional de Historia Natural de Chile
